title: Exit codes are the script contract
description: The exit status is how programs signal success or failure to shells, pipelines, and CI; 0 means success and non-zero means a specific failure.
body: Use when a CLI participates in scripts or CI. Shells branch on exit codes (`&&`, `||`, `if`, set -e) and CI marks a step failed on non-zero; the convention is 0 success, 1 general error, 2 misuse, and 126/127 for not-executable/not-found. Do not overload 0 to mean partial failure. Actions: assign and document distinct non-zero codes per failure class and always set the code before exit. Validation signals include shells correctly short-circuiting on failure. Risk boundary: returning 0 on failure defeats `set -e` and CI gates, letting broken steps pass silently — the exit code is a machine contract, not decoration.
